2604.10788.title: TInR: Exploring Tool-Internalized Reasoning in Large Language Models
2604.10788.paper_summary: Tool-Integrated Reasoning (TIR) has emerged as a promising direction by extending Large Language Models' (LLMs) capabilities with external tools during reasoning. Existing TIR methods typically rely on external tool documentation during reasoning. However, this leads to tool mastery difficulty, tool size constraints, and inference inefficiency. To mitigate these issues, we explore Tool-Internalized Reasoning (TInR), aiming at facilitating reasoning with tool knowledge internalized into LLMs. Achieving this goal presents notable requirements, including tool internalization and tool-reasoning coordination. To address them, we propose TInR-U, a tool-internalized reasoning framework for unified reasoning and tool usage. TInR-U is trained through a three-phase pipeline: 1) tool internalization with a bidirectional knowledge alignment strategy; 2) supervised fine-tuning warm-up using high-quality reasoning annotations, and 3) reinforcement learning with TInR-specific rewards. We comprehensively evaluate our method across in-domain and out-of-domain settings. Experiment results show that TInR-U achieves superior performance in both settings, highlighting its effectiveness and efficiency.
2604.10788.paper_summary_zh: 工具整合推理（TIR）已成為一個有前景的方向，通過在推理過程中擴展大型語言模型（LLMs）的能力與外部工具相結合。現有的TIR方法通常在推理過程中依賴外部工具文檔。然而，這導致了工具掌握的困難、工具大小的限制和推理效率的低下。為了緩解這些問題，我們探索了工具內化推理（TInR），旨在促進將工具知識內化到LLMs中的推理。實現這一目標需要顯著的要求，包括工具內化和工具推理協調。為了解決這些問題，我們提出了TInR-U，一個用於統一推理和工具使用的工具內化推理框架。TInR-U通過三階段的管道進行訓練：1）使用雙向知識對齊策略進行工具內化；2）使用高質量推理註釋進行監督性微調預熱；3）使用針對TInR的獎勵進行強化學習。我們在領域內和領域外的設置中全面評估我們的方法。實驗結果顯示，TInR-U在這兩種設置中都達到了優越的性能，突顯了其有效性和效率。
2604.10788.author: Qiancheng Xu et.al.
2604.10788.authors: Qiancheng Xu, Yongqi Li, Fan Liu, Hongru Wang, Min Yang, Wenjie Li
